questions.question: What is the fundamental logic function performed by a NAND gate?
options: It outputs true if any input is true, otherwise false (OR).
It outputs true if all inputs are true, otherwise false (AND).
It outputs true if at least one input is true, but not all inputs are true (XOR).
It outputs true only if all inputs are false, otherwise false (NOT-OR/NOR).
It outputs true only if at least one input is false, otherwise false (NOT-AND/NAND).

questions.question: In a general-purpose processor, what is the primary purpose of the Program Counter (PC)?
options: To store the result of the last arithmetic or logical operation.
To hold the address of the next instruction to be fetched from memory.
To contain the current instruction being executed by the CPU.
To store the base address of the data segment in memory.
To point to the top of the system stack for function calls.

questions.question: In the physical design flow of a digital integrated circuit, after initial placement and clock tree synthesis, what is the primary objective of the 'Routing' step?
options: To verify logical equivalence between the RTL design and the gate-level netlist.
To assign power and ground connections to all standard cells and macros.
To physically connect all pins of logic gates and macros according to the netlist using metal interconnects.
To optimize the clock distribution network for minimal skew and insertion delay across the chip.
To perform a comprehensive power consumption analysis of the entire design.

questions.question: In modern superscalar processors, which technique allows multiple independent instructions to be issued and executed in parallel within a single clock cycle by utilizing multiple execution units?
options: Pipelining, where instructions are overlapped in different stages of execution.
Clock Gating, which reduces dynamic power by disabling unused clock signals.
Instruction Level Parallelism (ILP), a measure of how many operations in a program can be executed simultaneously.
Dynamic Voltage Scaling (DVS), where the supply voltage is adjusted based on workload.
Cache Coherence, ensuring consistency of data in shared caches.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of using 'Assertions' (e.g., SystemVerilog Assertions - SVA) during the verification of a digital IC design?
options: To define physical layout constraints for component placement and routing.
To automatically generate directed test vectors for simulation-based verification.
To formally specify expected behavior and properties of the design, which can be dynamically checked during simulation or statically proven with formal verification tools.
To synthesize high-level behavioral code (RTL) into a gate-level netlist.
To calculate the optimal clock frequency and power consumption for the entire chip.
